pilgrammedCodes.name: Pilgrammed Codes
pilgrammedCodes.title: Pilgrammed Codes Status
pilgrammedCodes.subtitle: Pilgrammed does not have a live public code pool in April 2026, so this block works best as a status board and watchlist instead of a traditional redeem list.
pilgrammedCodes.intro: The latest code trackers all point to the same current state: no working public codes, no confirmed expired public codes, and no live redemption flow that players can actually use right now. The useful part of this module is tracking that status cleanly and pointing players to the official places where the first real drops would appear.

items.name: Working Codes
items.summary: No public working Pilgrammed codes are currently listed by the latest April 2026 trackers.
items.action: Keep this card visible as a live status tile until the first verified public code appears.

items.name: Expired Codes
items.summary: No confirmed expired public codes are being tracked either, which means Pilgrammed still has not settled into a normal public code cycle.
items.action: Use the expired section only after a code has been officially released and later retired.

items.name: Redeem Status
items.summary: The latest code guides say Pilgrammed does not currently have an active redemption feature for players to use.
items.action: Once a real redeem menu exists, replace this card with the in-game redeem steps.

items.name: Where To Watch
items.summary: The best places to monitor are the official Discord, the Phexonia Studios Roblox group, and major update-release windows tracked by the community wiki.
items.action: Surface those channels here first, then convert the module into a normal code list when the first verified drop lands.

pilgrammedBeginnerGuide.name: Pilgrammed Beginner Guide
pilgrammedBeginnerGuide.title: How to Start Pilgrammed After the Wind Update
pilgrammedBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Wind v1.6 changed movement, rebirth progression, and early routing, so the best beginner path now prioritizes mobility and safe progression before raw damage.
pilgrammedBeginnerGuide.intro: Pilgrammed is much easier once you stop treating the early game like a damage race. Unlock movement first, use quests and outposts to stabilize your route, and only then start pushing deeper for bosses, gear, and rebirth progress.

items.title: Finish the tutorial and use quests as your route
items.description: Clear the short tutorial, reach the first main town, and let quest markers guide your next fights instead of wandering into high-danger areas too early.

items.title: Get Dash from Garth immediately
items.description: Go to Garth in the Elite Barracks as soon as possible. Dash is the most important early mobility upgrade and unlocks proper dodging with short i-frame windows.

items.title: Unlock Climb, then start the jump route
items.description: Talk to Mela for Climb, then begin Adagio's questline for Jump Enhancement when your route opens up. Wind progression is built around permanent movement tools, not temporary consumables.

items.title: Learn tap parry before you try to brute-force fights
items.description: Tap F for a real parry instead of holding block. A clean parry staggers enemies, and once Dash is unlocked your AGI stat also improves movement, dash distance, and i-frame value.

items.title: Farm safe early XP and commit to one main stat lane
items.description: Deadly Swamp and Big Bay are the official beginner farming suggestions. Put your early points into STR for melee, DEX for ranged or fast weapons, or INT for magic-focused play.

items.title: Use every outpost like a reset station
items.description: Whenever you find an outpost, set your spawn, activate fast travel, deposit gold, sell extras, check quests, and update gear before going back out. That routine keeps deaths from turning into a gold drain.

items.title: Rebirth on time and start building Soul Levels
items.description: At level cap, rebirth instead of stalling. Each Soul Fragment adds +50% XP, five fragments buy one Soul Level, the cap is Level 13, and Psychocrab is not part of an efficient fragment route.

pilgrammedBestBuilds.name: Pilgrammed Best Builds
pilgrammedBestBuilds.title: Best Pilgrammed Builds Right Now
pilgrammedBestBuilds.subtitle: The current Wind meta is anchored by two flagship setups: a lifesteal-heavy melee build and a ranged burst build built around Catatome and Air Gun.
pilgrammedBestBuilds.intro: After the Legendary reforge removal, build strength comes from synergy more than rarity color. Patient, Sharp, and Cosmic now matter more than old purple-text prestige, and the public meta guide centers on two standout loadouts.

items.name: Immortal Scythe
items.setup: Blood Gem Haunted Scythe with Patient reforge, Prism Crown, Patchwork Armor, Diamond Ring, Sanguine Ring, Shark Tooth Necklace, and either Idol of Solitude for solo play or Combo Glove.
items.core_sequence: Stay on the target with the Scythe's heavy spin and weave in parries for Rally healing whenever the boss gives you a clean timing window.
items.why_it_works: The build layers Blood Gem lifesteal with Sanguine Ring sustain, letting the heavy spin out-heal most boss damage. Community testing also calls it the top Wind meta setup.
items.best_for: Bossing, solo clears, and players who want the most stable endgame melee setup.

items.name: Galvanized Artillery
items.setup: Air Gun with Insignia Gem and Shortsighted reforge, paired with Catatome, Prism Crown, Patchwork Armor, Outlaw Ammo Box, Sniper Scope, and Toolmark Quickloader.
items.core_sequence: Equip Catatome, switch to Mode 2, begin Cursed Fire, trigger Combustion Engine twice, activate Copper Mech, then swap instantly to Air Gun and fire into the burst window.
items.why_it_works: The Catatome interaction creates the documented Galvanized 20 burst window, which the public meta guide describes as the highest damage ceiling in the current patch.
items.best_for: Burst phases, boss punish windows, and players comfortable with fast inputs, animation timing, and weapon swapping.

pilgrammedWeaponTierList.name: Pilgrammed Weapon Tier List
pilgrammedWeaponTierList.title: Current Pilgrammed Weapon Tier List
pilgrammedWeaponTierList.subtitle: The April 2026 post-Wind list is built around correct reforge choice and build synergy, not old rarity color.
pilgrammedWeaponTierList.intro: S tier is led by Blood Gem Haunted Scythe, Black Stiletto, and Catatome, while A tier adds powerful specialists like Bigfish Gauntlet, Bone Claws, Magma Shackles, and Air Gun.

items.title: Current Meta Staples
entries: Blood Gem Haunted Scythe — STR, 95 base damage, 0.7 slow attack speed. Patient reforge plus lifesteal makes it the core of the Immortal Scythe build.
Black Stiletto — DEX, 88 base damage, 1.6 fast attack speed. It has the highest crit multiplier in the list and wants Sharp reforge.
Catatome — INT, 82 base damage, 1.0 medium attack speed. Its four modes and Galvanized synergy keep it in S tier.

items.title: Strong Picks With Clear Roles
entries: Bigfish Gauntlet — STR, 75 base damage, 0.6 very slow attack speed. It excels during boss stagger windows because of its huge poise damage.
Bone Claws — DEX, 52 base damage, 2.0 very fast attack speed. The patch notes call out its low endlag and hit-and-run strength.
Magma Shackles — STR fire fists, 70 base damage, 0.9 medium attack speed. It is strong but feels best when paired with Magma Shell.
Air Gun — DEX firearm, 65 base damage, 1.2 medium attack speed. It is an A-tier weapon by itself and a meta core when used inside Galvanized Artillery.

items.title: Useful, But More Matchup-Dependent
entries: Tektite Crusher — STR maul, 68 base damage, 0.5 very slow attack speed. It is built for anti-defense pressure against armored targets.
Tektite Ballista — DEX crossbow, 72 base damage, 0.4 very slow attack speed. The reload is long, but the piercing burst is strong in punish windows.
Scrap Lance — STR polearm, 45 base damage, 1.1 medium attack speed. Its reach and Billy vendor access make it a practical mid-tier progression pick.
Elf Blade — DEX light sword, 42 base damage, 1.5 fast attack speed. It is a reliable early-mid DEX weapon from Fang's vendor line.

items.title: Early Fillers And Transition Tools
entries: Pitch Dagger — DEX, 35 base damage, 1.8 fast attack speed. Its main long-term value is trading into Black Stiletto at Strange Chasm.
Wooden Bow — DEX light bow, 18 base damage, 0.8 second charge. It is an early-game bow that should be replaced quickly once better ranged options appear.
Standard Starter Weapons — serviceable only at the start, with the tier list treating them as temporary tools rather than real meta choices.

pilgrammedBestWeapons.name: Pilgrammed Best Weapons
pilgrammedBestWeapons.title: Pilgrammed Best Weapons
pilgrammedBestWeapons.subtitle: A stage-by-stage shortlist for the current Wind meta, from easy pickup options to endgame carry weapons.
pilgrammedBestWeapons.intro: These are the weapons with the best payoff for the least wasted time right now. The list favors weapons with strong current rankings, clear acquisition paths, and obvious build identities instead of trying to rank every weapon in the game.

items.best_for: Safe spacing and low-risk early progression
items.why_it_matters: Very accessible and gives superior reach for learning enemy patterns without standing point-blank.

items.acquisition: Purchased from Fang on the Deadly Swamp-to-Lighthouse path
items.best_for: Reliable DEX sword progression
items.why_it_matters: A clean early pickup for players who want a light weapon with better reach than a dagger.

items.best_for: Hit-and-run DEX builds
items.why_it_matters: The current patch's lowest endlag option among top picks, making it excellent for constant pressure and quick disengages.

items.best_for: Burst-focused ranged builds
items.why_it_matters: The core weapon of the Galvanized Artillery setup and one of the strongest ranged payoff weapons in the current Wind meta.

items.acquisition: Trade Pitch Dagger to the black bird at Strange Chasm
items.best_for: Critical-hit melee burst
items.why_it_matters: Its critical multiplier is the main reason DEX crit builds remain one of the fastest boss-kill routes in the patch.

items.acquisition: Boss drop or crafting with Blood Gem
items.best_for: Solo bossing and sustain-heavy melee
items.why_it_matters: It is the centerpiece of the Immortal Scythe setup, combining heavy-spin damage with enough lifesteal pressure to survive most boss fights.

items.best_for: INT builds and hybrid burst setups
items.why_it_matters: Its four firing modes make it one of the most flexible weapons in the game, and it also feeds current hybrid ranged setups.

pilgrammedWindUpdateNotes.name: Pilgrammed Wind Update Notes
pilgrammedWindUpdateNotes.title: Pilgrammed Wind Update Notes
pilgrammedWindUpdateNotes.subtitle: Version 1.6 on April 10, 2026 rebuilt progression, movement, weapon balance, and several boss fights.
pilgrammedWindUpdateNotes.intro: Wind is the biggest rules rewrite Pilgrammed has had in a long time. These sections focus on the changes players feel immediately when they log in, rebuild a character, or remake a loadout.

items.section: Progression was rebuilt around Soul Levels
items.summary: The old late-game flow was replaced with permanent post-rebirth progression.
bullets: Soul Levels were added with a cap of Level 13.
Each rebirth now grants 1 Soul Fragment and a +50% XP bonus per fragment.
Each Soul Level costs 5 Soul Fragments, so Level 13 takes 65 fragments total.
Each level adds +10 HP and +2 Mana.

items.summary: Traversal is now something you unlock and keep, not a temporary side perk.
bullets: Dash is obtained from Garth in the Elite Barracks.
Climb is obtained from Mela.
Jump Enhancement is tied to Adagio's questline.
The Empyrean Knight obstacle course adds a second air jump and an air dive extension.
Scurry Boots and Cloud Boots now dash in the direction of the camera.

items.section: Legendary was removed and reforge economics changed
items.summary: The patch killed the old purple-text chase and pushed players toward modifier synergy instead.
bullets: Legendary reforge was permanently removed from acquisition pools.
Existing purple-tier Legendary weapons were downgraded on patch day.
Talvern's reforge costs now scale by guild rank, with a 15% discount at Novice and up to 75% at Legend.
Gem removal at Legend rank drops to 25 Gold.

items.section: The patch added new weapons, gear, vendors, and a new island
items.summary: Wind did not just rebalance the old game; it also expanded the item route map.
bullets: New weapons include Bigfish Gauntlet, Bone Claws, Tektite Crusher, Tektite Ballista, Catatome, Magma Shackles, Black Stiletto, and Scrap Lance.
New accessories include Dancer's Necklace, Husk Armor, Icy Tear, Rotten Trowel, and Expert.
Fang was added on the Deadly Swamp-to-Lighthouse path and sells Elf Blade, Pitch Dagger, Dagger Sheath, and more.
Toolmark was added on Tailfish Island and sells Quickloader, Magic Token, and the Expert accessory.
Tailfish Island opened as a new Western Sea location.

items.section: Bows and weapon techniques were overhauled
items.summary: Ranged combat now has a much more active identity than before.
bullets: Bow damage now scales from 0.5x at minimum draw to 1.1x at full draw.
All bows now share a jumping-kick heavy attack that deals 10 damage, adds knockback, and gives i-frames.
New quivers include Bottomless Quiver, Sawtooth Quiver, Augmented Gel Quiver, and Elemental Quiver.
New techniques include Spreadshot, Dagger Throw, Twisted Technique, and Duelist's Dagger.

items.section: Bosses and status effects changed sharply
items.summary: Several major encounters now play very differently from older guides.
bullets: Ice King received a full AI rewrite.
Magma Turtle is now a two-phase fight, and its Phase 2 Head Slam is unblockable and deals 60 damage.
Shock Marauder's high-HP shot is now more clearly telegraphed and strictly parryable.
Snow Cultist now scales defense harder in multiplayer and gains a Phase 2 Backstab at 50% HP.
Bleed was shifted away from being primary damage and is now more about debuff utility.

items.section: Launch polish and hotfixes arrived immediately
items.summary: A few systems were cleaned up within hours of release.
bullets: Experimental projectile detection was added for better hit registration on high-velocity shots.
Anti-Eye-Strain Mode was added to reduce overlapping particles and harsh bloom.
The Expert accessory's infinite-arrow duplication exploit was hotfixed.
Catatome mode-lock desync was identified, with unequip and re-equip as the current workaround.

pilgrammedReforgeGuide.name: Pilgrammed Reforge Guide
pilgrammedReforgeGuide.title: Pilgrammed Reforge Guide
pilgrammedReforgeGuide.subtitle: Use reforges to shape a weapon around its real job instead of chasing old rarity color.
pilgrammedReforgeGuide.intro: Reforging changes the modifier on a weapon or tool, adding both buffs and tradeoffs. In current Wind play, Talvern at Eggroost Observatory is the practical reforge hub because he also handles gems, and his guild-rank discount can scale all the way to 75% with gem removal dropping to 25 Gold at Legend rank.

items.effect: The old Legendary modifier is no longer obtainable.
items.best_for: Every build
items.notes: Legendary used to give +10% Swing Speed and +10% Damage with no downside, but Wind removed it from acquisition and forced old Legendary gear down a tier.

items.best_for: Crit-first melee and close-range burst
items.notes: This is the most direct fit for Black Stiletto and similar weapons that want to stay glued to the target instead of launching it away.

items.best_for: Big stagger windows and hard single-hit punishes
items.notes: Take this on weapons that already lean into slow, high-commitment hits and care more about impact than swing tempo.

items.effect: +15% Damage; -10% Max Range, -20% Velocity, -50% Min Range
items.best_for: Air Gun style close-to-mid burst play
items.notes: Current Galvanized Artillery setups run Air Gun with Shortsighted because the raw damage gain matters more than long-range comfort.

items.target: Long-range bows or cleaner gun shots
items.effect: +20% Max Range, -50% Spread, +50% Velocity, +10% Crit Chance; +20% Startup, +20% Cooldown
items.best_for: Safer ranged play and cleaner shot consistency
items.notes: Pick this when you want reliable distance control rather than point-blank damage spikes.

items.best_for: Zoning, crowd control, and wider hitboxes
items.notes: This is the size-first magic option for players who want easier coverage and more forgiving projectile or spell area.

items.effect: -10% Mana Cost, +20% Damage, +30% Time; +20% Startup, +20% Cooldown
items.best_for: Slower, higher-payoff casts and extended damage windows
items.notes: In current Wind meta coverage, Patient is also the named pairing for the Blood Gem Haunted Scythe lifesteal setup.

pilgrammedSoulLevelGuide.name: Pilgrammed Soul Level Guide
pilgrammedSoulLevelGuide.title: Pilgrammed Soul Level Guide
pilgrammedSoulLevelGuide.subtitle: Soul Levels are the permanent post-cap progression system added in Wind and they now define long-term character growth.
pilgrammedSoulLevelGuide.intro: The system becomes simple once the numbers are visible. Rebirth for fragments, cash in five fragments for one Soul Level, and use the early milestones to decide when to push harder bosses instead of wandering into them under-statted.

items.heading: Start the system with a rebirth
items.body: Each rebirth now gives 1 Soul Fragment. That fragment also adds a +50% XP bonus, and the XP bonus stacks up to five fragments for faster follow-up progression.

items.heading: Trade fragments for permanent levels
items.body: Soul Levels are capped at 13. Because every level costs 5 fragments, the full route to cap requires 65 fragments in total.

items.heading: Know what every level gives you
items.body: Soul Levels are steady permanent growth. Going from Level 0 to Level 13 gives a total of +130 HP and +26 Mana before any extra bonuses from skills or gear.

items.heading: Use the first real milestone before hard bosses
items.body: Current Wind progression guides recommend hitting Soul Level 5 before committing to bosses such as Old Fart, N, or Snow Cultist.

items.heading: Farm where the system pays out fastest
items.body: The recommended fragment route is to farm mob clusters in Deadly Swamp or Big Bay. Psychocrab is specifically excluded from fragment drops, so it is a bad stop for Soul progression.

items.heading: Understand your cap totals before you plan gear
items.body: The current calculator uses 280 base HP and 50 base Mana before Soul Levels. At cap, HP reaches 410 and mana gains total +26 on top of base.

pilgrammedSkillTreeGuide.name: Pilgrammed Skill Tree Guide

layout.desktop: two-column accordion with nested node cards

pilgrammedSkillTreeGuide.title: Pilgrammed Skill Tree Guide: Best Skill Packages by Build Type
pilgrammedSkillTreeGuide.subtitle: Use these grouped skill packages to respec faster and match your tree to your weapon style.
pilgrammedSkillTreeGuide.intro: Pilgrammed rewards focused trees more than spread-out point spending. These build packages turn the raw offense, defense, and misc skills into practical loadouts for crit melee, bows, magic, boss survival, and the current Wind meta heavy-melee setup.

items.title: Wind Meta Scythe
items.summary: The current Advanced Meta guide presents this as the dominant heavy-melee lifesteal setup for Wind-era Pilgrammed.

main_nodes.name: Delicate
main_nodes.base: Gain 2% crit chance for every defense point below 10, up to 20%.
main_nodes.aced: Gain 1% increased crit damage for every defense point below 10, up to 15%.

main_nodes.name: Vexation
main_nodes.base: Max health is decreased by 50% and non-natural healing is reduced by 25%, but you gain 25% damage reduction.
main_nodes.aced: Damage reduction becomes 50% and non-natural healing reduction becomes 20%.

main_nodes.name: Rally
main_nodes.base: Parrying an attack increases mana regeneration by 25% for 3 seconds.
main_nodes.aced: Parrying an attack restores 6% of HP over 2 seconds.

optional_nodes.name: Athlete
optional_nodes.base: Reach max running speed 50% faster and increase max running speed by 10%.

optional_nodes.name: Fearmonger
optional_nodes.base: Taking damage makes enemies you hit fearful for 5 seconds, so they take 10% more damage and deal 10% less damage.
optional_nodes.aced: Fear effects become twice as effective.

items.takeaway: The Advanced Meta guide lists Delicate Aced, Vexation Aced, Rally Aced, plus an Athlete or Fearmonger base tree as the core skill package for the Immortal Scythe build.

items.title: Low-Defense Crit Melee
items.summary: A fast melee package for daggers, rapiers, and other styles that want crit value without investing into defense.

main_nodes.name: Delicate
main_nodes.base: Gain 2% crit chance for every defense point below 10, up to 20%.
main_nodes.aced: Gain 1% increased crit damage for every defense point below 10, up to 15%.

main_nodes.name: Athlete
main_nodes.base: Reach max running speed 50% faster and increase max running speed by 10%.

main_nodes.name: Slippery
main_nodes.base: Reduce the movement speed penalty from attacking or being stunned by 35%.
main_nodes.aced: Dodging an attack with a roll or dive resets your roll cooldown.

items.takeaway: Stay under 10 DEF to maximize Delicate, then lean on Athlete and Slippery to keep pressure and dodge uptime high.

items.title: Bow and Gun Pressure
items.summary: A ranged package built around charge timing, repositioning, and not losing your whole turn to a stun.

main_nodes.name: Archery
main_nodes.base: Hit a target within 1.5 seconds of fully charging your bow to build crit chance, up to 25%.
main_nodes.aced: The same bonus can also reduce charge time by up to 50% and no longer disappears entirely on failure.

main_nodes.name: Athlete
main_nodes.base: Reach max running speed 50% faster and increase max running speed by 10%.

main_nodes.name: Focus
main_nodes.base: Being stunned no longer stops you from rolling.
main_nodes.aced: Being stunned no longer stops you from blocking or parrying.

items.takeaway: Archery rewards consistent charge-and-hit timing, while Athlete and Focus make kiting and recovery much cleaner under pressure.

items.title: Close-Range Mage
items.summary: A spell package for players who cast near enemies instead of playing at maximum distance.

main_nodes.name: Wizardry
main_nodes.base: Being very close to an enemy increases mana gained by up to 25%.
main_nodes.aced: The range increases by 50% and the boost rises to 40%.

main_nodes.name: Rally
main_nodes.base: Parrying an attack increases mana regeneration by 25% for 3 seconds.
main_nodes.aced: Parrying an attack restores 6% of HP over 2 seconds.

main_nodes.name: Focus
main_nodes.base: Being stunned no longer stops you from rolling.
main_nodes.aced: Being stunned no longer stops you from blocking or parrying.

items.takeaway: Wizardry rewards staying in the fight, while Rally and Focus help you keep mana and defensive control during close-range casting windows.

items.title: Frontline Bruiser
items.summary: A survivability-first package for slow weapons, party frontline duty, and boss learning runs.

main_nodes.name: Juggernaut
main_nodes.base: While in combat, gain 10% damage reduction but lose 10% speed and draw more enemy aggro.
main_nodes.aced: Gain another 10% damage reduction and 10% increased damage, but lose another 10% movement speed.

main_nodes.name: Resilient
main_nodes.base: Taking damage grants stacking 10% damage resistance up to 50% for 2 seconds.
main_nodes.aced: The effect lasts 3 seconds and also grants super armor when you take damage.

items.takeaway: This package trades speed for consistency, then uses Tank and Resilient to smooth out repeated hits during longer fights.

items.title: Dodge-Heavy Bossing
items.summary: A movement-first package for fights where repeated dodges matter more than standing your ground.

main_nodes.name: Athlete
main_nodes.base: Reach max running speed 50% faster and increase max running speed by 10%.

main_nodes.name: Slippery
main_nodes.base: Reduce the movement speed penalty from attacking or being stunned by 35%.
main_nodes.aced: Dodging an attack with a roll or dive resets your roll cooldown.

main_nodes.name: Focus
main_nodes.base: Being stunned no longer stops you from rolling.
main_nodes.aced: Being stunned no longer stops you from blocking or parrying.

items.takeaway: Use this when the safest answer is repeated repositioning. The skill pages specifically call out Slippery for N-style dodge fights, and N's boss page highlights Athlete Aced as almost essential.

pilgrammedStatsGuide.name: Pilgrammed Stats Guide
pilgrammedStatsGuide.title: Pilgrammed Stats Guide: How STR, DEX, INT, DEF, and AGI Actually Work
pilgrammedStatsGuide.subtitle: A clean lookup table for the five character stats that shape melee, ranged, magic, defense, and movement.
pilgrammedStatsGuide.intro: Character stats sit on top of your weapon stats and decide whether your build wins through melee damage, ranged pressure, spell output, survivability, or mobility. Most of these stat effects scale in 0.5% steps, so stacking the right stat matters much more than spreading points evenly.

items.exact_effect: Each point adds 0.5% more melee damage.
items.secondary_effects: Also boosts Thorn Ring, Thief King Cloak's ram, Bear Flute's summon, and several fishing actions.
items.best_for: Swords, daggers, greatswords, and any build whose main damage comes from swings
items.practical_pick: Take STR first when your main weapon spends more time swinging than casting or shooting.

items.primary_role: Ranged damage and melee speed stat
items.exact_effect: Raises ranged weapon damage and increases melee swing speed by 0.5% per point.
items.secondary_effects: Also shortens fishing cast time and helps with slippery fish catches.
items.best_for: Bows, guns, and fast melee hybrids that care about attack tempo
items.practical_pick: DEX is the cleanest choice when you want ranged damage without giving up attack speed on backup melee weapons.

items.exact_effect: Positive INT raises magic weapon damage and max mana; negative INT lowers both.
items.secondary_effects: Also reduces fishing wait time and improves treasure chest odds.
items.best_for: Staves, mana-heavy builds, and magic-first loadouts
items.practical_pick: If your damage loop costs mana, INT is the stat that keeps both output and resource pool growing together.

items.exact_effect: Adds flat resistance to incoming damage and also increases damage reduction by 0.2% per point.
items.secondary_effects: Negative DEF increases damage taken, and piercing can ignore part of your protection.
items.best_for: Frontliners, learning fights, and slower builds that expect to trade hits
items.practical_pick: DEF is the safest stat when you are still learning boss patterns or want more room for mistakes.

items.exact_effect: Improves movement speed, dash distance, iframe length, and gun reload speed; reload speed rises by 0.75% per point.
items.secondary_effects: Negative AGI lowers speed and shortens your iframe windows.
items.best_for: Kiting, dodge-heavy bossing, guns, and mobility-first play
items.practical_pick: AGI is the best quality-of-life stat when your build lives or dies on spacing, reload timing, and clean dodges.

pilgrammedBossGuide.name: Pilgrammed Boss Guide
pilgrammedBossGuide.title: Pilgrammed Boss Guide: Major Bosses, Drops, and Danger Points
pilgrammedBossGuide.subtitle: Use this as a fast boss-by-boss sheet for loot priority, danger points, and farming value.
pilgrammedBossGuide.intro: Pilgrammed bosses are progression checks and loot farms at the same time. This guide focuses on what each fight gives you, why players return to it, and the single mechanic most likely to end a run. In the current Wind routing, Old Fart, N, and Snow Cultist are treated as endgame checkpoints, while Psychocrab is explicitly not a Soul Fragment leveling stop.

items.title: Ice King
items.summary: A sleeping frost boss with readable telegraphs and guaranteed frost-themed drops.
worth_it_for: Reliable frost gear farming in the snow region
Fast repeat attempts because the boss stays asleep until you choose to start the fight
watch_for: Icicle Stomp is the real danger because the stomp and frost lines can stack into a very large hit
His block stance punishes careless pressure by spawning icicles near your position
items.fight_note: Found west of The Snow Highlands below Mountain Peak.

items.title: Magma Turtle
items.summary: A Volcano boss built around fire pressure, shell durability, and a clear head-slam stun window.
watch_for: Head Slam is the main stun window if you parry it
At 50% HP the fight adds Meteor Shower and becomes much harder to stand still in
items.fight_note: Found on the east side of Volcano in the remains of a destroyed village.

items.title: Shock Marauder
items.summary: A lightning gunslinger with one of the most valuable guaranteed Suslands loot pools.
drops: Sundowners or Shock Bullets or Storm Boots
worth_it_for: Excellent guaranteed drop table because every clear yields a weapon, ammo piece, or movement item
Storm Boots alone makes the fight worth learning for many movement-focused players
watch_for: High Noon is the boss's main punish window because parrying it stuns him
Teleport chains and lock-on gun attacks punish panic rolling
items.fight_note: Rare spawn in The Suslands; after Viperhead, Verdant sells Copper Crystal in Stonesurgeon to summon it.

items.title: Snow Cultist
items.summary: A tight-arena Frost duel that drops a guaranteed weapon roll and Chaos Essence.
worth_it_for: Guaranteed endgame-tier weapon result from Snowflurry or Snowshatters
Repeatable Chaos Essence farming once the route is mastered
watch_for: The fight is packed into a small arena and uses teleports, lunges, and fast strings
At 50% HP the boss transitions into a new phase built around Backstab and harder burst windows
The current Wind routing treats Snow Cultist as an endgame target rather than an early farm
items.fight_note: Found at the end of Frozen Cathedral.

items.title: Psychocrab
items.summary: A hidden secret boss whose main gimmick is that your damage is bad until you poise break it.
worth_it_for: Cavern material farming when you specifically want Iridescent Shards
watch_for: Cyrus' Shield massively suppresses your damage until the boss is poise broken
The hidden arena and psychic cue-heavy moves punish tunnel vision
items.fight_note: The current Wind guide specifically says Psychocrab drops zero Soul Fragments, so it is not part of the efficient leveling route.

items.title: Old Fart
items.summary: A long nightmare fight with heavy chaos payouts, screen disruption, and brutal phase mechanics.
worth_it_for: One of the key late-game mushroom and chaos farms
watch_for: Old Man's Rose can drop your health to 1 if the fight drags on
Later phases add beams, walls, floor tiles, and screen effects that break rhythm
Access is gated because the Field only opens after three purple sigils are broken
items.fight_note: The Wind routing treats Old Fart as an endgame boss.

items.title: N
items.summary: A quest-gated chaos boss with a very strong weapon drop pool and one of the best Chaos Essence payouts.
worth_it_for: A huge direct Chaos Essence payout
Guaranteed weapon result from a boss with no defense stat
A major late-game target once Chaos Hideout is unlocked
watch_for: The fight is teleport-heavy and gets harder after 50% HP
Poise damage helps a lot because N can be stunned repeatedly
The boss page specifically recommends Athlete Aced and/or Celestial Ring for the dodge timing
items.fight_note: Quest-gated through Innocuous Induction and summoned at the top of Chaos Hideout.

pilgrammedBossLocations.name: Pilgrammed Boss Locations
pilgrammedBossLocations.title: Pilgrammed Boss Locations: Faster Landmark Routes to Major Bosses
pilgrammedBossLocations.subtitle: A route-first locator for the major bosses players most often search by name plus location.
pilgrammedBossLocations.intro: These route cards turn the interactive map and boss pages into quick travel hints. Each entry tells you which region to enter, what landmark to aim for, and whether the fight is static, hidden, or summon-based so you can move straight from search intent to navigation.

items.arena: West side of The Snow Highlands, below Mountain Peak
items.fast_route: Enter the Snow Highlands boss zone and keep moving west until you are below Mountain Peak.
items.spawn_method: Static boss that sleeps until attacked
notes: Because the boss starts asleep, you can set up your opener before aggroing it

items.arena: East side of Volcano, inside the ruins of a destroyed village
items.fast_route: Push east through Volcano until you reach the ruined settlement; the boss arena is there.
notes: Also appears in The Big Iron and Sky Battlegrounds

items.arena: Rare natural spawn in the zone or forced summon near your position
items.fast_route: For the reliable route, defeat Viperhead first, then buy a Copper Crystal from Verdant in Stonesurgeon and use it to summon the fight.
notes: The summon route is far more consistent than waiting for a natural spawn

items.arena: At the far end of Frozen Cathedral
items.fast_route: In The Winter, drop through the breakable snow hole, go west across the small islands, reach the two-way island, take the right path, and continue to the cathedral entrance.
items.spawn_method: Static boss at the cathedral end
notes: Using a Glider helps on the approach
The boss is already spinning in place when you arrive

items.fast_route: Go behind one of the waterfalls in Cobalt Cavern, enter the hidden cave with the golden chest, then break the false wall behind that chest with a pickaxe or weapon.
notes: This is one of the easiest bosses to miss because the path is hidden behind scenery

items.fast_route: In Morning Hollow, find Condemned Passage opposite the route to Forgotten Village, break the plank hiding the crevice, then unlock the Field by breaking three purple sigils around the map.
items.spawn_method: Static once the Field is opened
notes: The gate stays locked until all three sigils are broken

items.arena: Top floor of Chaos Hideout on the southeast cliff of Volcano
items.fast_route: Complete Innocuous Induction, reach Chaos Hideout either by climbing the southeast Volcano cliffs or by coming through Deep Desert Caverns, then climb to the top floor and destroy the sigil on the center table.
notes: This is a quest-gated route, not a simple overworld walk-up

pilgrammedUpgradesGuide.name: Pilgrammed Upgrades Guide
pilgrammedUpgradesGuide.title: Pilgrammed Upgrades Guide: Dash, Climb, Jump, and Let's Fly
pilgrammedUpgradesGuide.subtitle: Build Pilgrammed movement in the right order so every later route becomes safer, faster, and easier to recover.
pilgrammedUpgradesGuide.intro: Pilgrammed feels dramatically better once mobility is handled first. This sequence gives you the core dodge unlock, the wall route, the vertical booster, and the final aerial extension without wasting time on gear detours.

items.unlock: Talk to Garth in the Elite Barracks.
items.effect: Unlocks your dash-based evasive movement; Agility affects dash distance and i-frame value.
items.why_get_it_early: This is the most important early survival tool for spacing, dodging, and general route speed.

items.unlock: Talk to Mela for the Climb skill.
items.effect: Lets you scale environmental surfaces and use up to three wall-climb actions instead of taking long legacy ground routes.
items.why_get_it_early: It opens shortcuts, safer pathing, and faster access to vertical zones.

items.effect: Improves jump height and distance for cleaner platforming and recovery.
items.why_get_it_early: It smooths out exploration, makes route mistakes less punishing, and prepares you for the advanced mobility chain.

items.unlock: Use the teleporter on the Empyrean Knight island and clear the glider course through the white atmospheric orbs.
items.effect: Adds a secondary air jump and extends your air dive.
items.why_get_it_early: This is the highest-value traversal upgrade for hidden routes, aerial correction, and late exploration.

pilgrammedProgressionGuide.name: Pilgrammed Progression Guide
pilgrammedProgressionGuide.title: Pilgrammed Progression Guide: Tutorial to Midgame Route
pilgrammedProgressionGuide.subtitle: This Pilgrammed roadmap turns the open world into a clean checklist: mobility first, stable XP second, outpost habits third, Soul Level farming after.
pilgrammedProgressionGuide.intro: Pilgrammed progression is smoother when every phase has one job. Finish the intro, secure movement, build a mixed kit, farm the right zones, use every outpost correctly, then convert rebirths into permanent Soul Levels.

items.focus: Finish the tutorial and stabilize your loadout
checklist: Finish the tutorial area and reach the first main town.
Slot at least one melee option and keep ranged or magic ready when possible.
Practice parry timing and hit-and-run movement instead of face-tanking.
items.payoff: You enter the open world with enough tools to handle mixed enemy patterns.

items.focus: Get mobility before you chase levels
checklist: Talk to Garth in the Elite Barracks for Dash.
Get Climb from Mela.
Use these two unlocks before committing to longer exploration routes.
items.payoff: Combat and world traversal both become dramatically safer.

items.focus: Prep for your first real boss and early quests
items.route: Nearby early zones and quest markers
checklist: Follow quest markers when you are unsure where to go next.
Carry a mixed kit instead of forcing one class only.
For early bossing, a melee weapon plus Magic Branch plus Wooden Bow is a reliable beginner setup.
items.payoff: You gain XP, special items, and a much more natural zone order.

checklist: Start farming mobs in Deadly Swamp or Big Bay.
Put your stat points into the style you actually want to scale.
Use quests to bridge any gear or level gap instead of forcing difficult fights.
items.payoff: These areas are the standard early leveling route into midgame equipment farming.

items.focus: Do the full outpost routine every time
checklist: Set your spawn point if needed.
Activate the fast-travel mirror.
Deposit your money, sell extras, buy upgrades, and check for new quests.
Adjust skills, loadouts, and reforges before heading back out.
items.payoff: You lose less gold on death and keep your routes much shorter.

items.route: Level cap -> rebirth -> Soul farming loop
checklist: Once you hit the level cap, do your first rebirth.
Stack 5 Soul Fragments and trade them for Soul Level 1.
Aim for Soul Level 5 before pushing late bosses like Old Fart, N, or Snow Cultist.
Skip Psychocrab on Soul Fragment routes because it drops none.
items.payoff: Soul Level 5 already means +50 HP and +10 Mana, which makes mid-to-late progression noticeably easier.

pilgrammedFishingGuide.name: Pilgrammed Fishing Guide
pilgrammedFishingGuide.title: Pilgrammed Fishing Guide: Gold, Materials, and Jared Milestones
pilgrammedFishingGuide.subtitle: Pilgrammed fishing is a full economy track once you understand rod progression, bait targeting, and Jared's milestone rewards.
pilgrammedFishingGuide.intro: Open with Jared in Big Bay, move through the rod upgrades, and treat bait choice like route planning. Once Rod of Kings and Secret Spore are online, Tailfish Island becomes a dedicated Pilgrammed gold engine.

items.name: How Fishing Works
items.details: Cast into water with a fishing rod, wait for the bobber splash, then reel in by clicking. Fishing can yield fish materials, Treasure Chests, Rotten Divers, Bigfish, and Sharks.
items.best_for: Players learning the mechanic
items.watch_out_for: While fishing, you move slowly and cannot run, roll, jump, dive, or block.

items.name: Starter Rod
items.details: Jared sells the basic Fishing Rod in Big Bay. Craft cost: 200 Gold, 10 Red Berry, 10 Blue Berry, and 3 Iron.
items.best_for: Unlocking fishing and starting Jared's quest chain

items.name: Rod Progression
items.details: Advanced Rod upgrades the base rod with Range 30 and +60% Lure. Rod of Kings is the end goal with Range 35 and +120% Lure.
items.best_for: Faster reels and better long-session farming
items.how_to_unlock: Advanced Rod is crafted via Jared. Rod of Kings is awarded every 20 Fishing Quests.

items.name: Best Baits by Goal
items.details: Secret Spore increases luck and improves Treasure Chest odds. Rage Berry cuts waiting time the most. Gelatinous Sludge has the lowest consumption rate and the highest chance to pull hostile catches like Rotten Divers and Sharks.
items.best_for: Choosing whether you want profit, speed, or enemy-based drops
top_picks: Rage Berry for faster general fishing
Gelatinous Sludge for low-consumption and hostile-catch farming

items.name: Jared's Fishing Quests
items.details: Fishing Quests give Gold, Ores, and Gems. Every 5 quests, Jared offers one of three random milestone rewards; every 20 quests, Rod of Kings is guaranteed.
items.best_for: Turning fishing into long-term progression

items.name: Current Best Gold Route
items.details: The strongest confirmed route is Rod of Kings plus Secret Spore bait at High Activity Spots on Tailfish Island. Alga Trout and Salt Eel are the key value catches, and Rotten Divers in the Western Sea can supplement Salt Eel drops.
items.best_for: Players moving from side activity into real economy farming
items.upgrade_path: Finish 20 Jared milestones first, then move your dedicated fishing sessions to Tailfish Island.

pilgrammedArmorGuide.name: Pilgrammed Armor Guide
pilgrammedArmorGuide.title: Pilgrammed Armor Guide: Best Sets by Build Type
pilgrammedArmorGuide.subtitle: The best Pilgrammed armor pieces are the ones that reinforce your weapon loop, not the ones with the biggest defense number.
pilgrammedArmorGuide.intro: Compare armor by passive payoff first. These Pilgrammed sets cover bossing, crit sustain, Flintlock burst, mana-threshold casting, dagger super-crit routing, and movement-heavy exploration.

items.how_to_get: Treasure Chest in Northern Labyrinth, or buy/craft through Betsy after joining Elite Knights.
items.signature_effect: With the Viking Helmet, you deal 15% more damage to bosses.
items.notable_stats: +20 STR, +15 DEF, -4 AGI on the armor
items.why_use_it: This is the cleanest straightforward melee bossing set for players who want damage without gimmicks.

items.how_to_get: Buy or craft it through Betsy in Elite Barracks after reaching Journeyman in Elite Knights.
items.signature_effect: Critical hits heal 20% of crit damage dealt, plus 1 flat HP.
items.notable_stats: +25 DEX, +8 AGI, +10% Crit Chance, -5 DEF, -15 INT, -75% Regen
items.why_use_it: Patchwork is still one of the most important crit-centered armors and remains relevant in Wind-era high-end builds.

items.signature_effect: With Pirate Hat equipped, Flintlock fires 3 bullets in an arc while consuming only 1 ammo.
items.why_use_it: This is the signature armor choice for dedicated Flintlock setups and one of the clearest ranged synergies in the game.

items.signature_effect: Mage Weapons deal 30% more damage when you are below 25% of your maximum mana.
items.why_use_it: Diver rewards mana management and gives one of the strongest direct magic damage passives on armor.

items.signature_effect: After 6 dagger hits, the next heavy attack becomes a guaranteed super crit.
items.notable_stats: +12 DEX, +5 AGI, +10% Crit Chance, -20% Detection Risk
items.why_use_it: Rogue is the dedicated dagger armor and keeps early-to-midgame DEX builds efficient without overcomplication.

items.how_to_get: Crafted by Simon on Pale Island.
items.notable_stats: +15 STR, +8 DEF, +4 AGI, +15% Regen, +10% Max Speed
items.why_use_it: Night Count is excellent for movement-heavy routes, night exploration, and players who value travel comfort as much as combat.

faq.title: Pilgrammed
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Pilgrammed codes, progression, updates, and core systems.

questions.question: Does Pilgrammed have active public codes right now?
questions.answer: No. Pilgrammed does not have a live public code pool right now, so the best move is to watch the official Discord, Roblox group, and major update windows.

questions.question: How should I start Pilgrammed after the Wind update?
questions.answer: Start Pilgrammed by finishing the tutorial, getting Dash from Garth, unlocking Climb from Mela, and using quests plus outposts to control your route before chasing hard bosses.

questions.question: What is the best Pilgrammed build in the current meta?
questions.answer: The current Pilgrammed meta revolves around the Immortal Scythe heavy-melee setup and the Galvanized Artillery burst-ranged setup, with each build excelling in a different punish window.

questions.question: How do Pilgrammed Soul Levels work?
questions.answer: Pilgrammed Soul Levels come from rebirth fragments. Five fragments buy one Soul Level, the cap is 13, and every level adds permanent HP and Mana.

questions.question: Which Pilgrammed upgrades matter the most early on?
questions.answer: Dash, Climb, Jump Enhancement, and Let's Fly are the highest-value Pilgrammed upgrades because they reshape combat safety, routing, and hidden-path access.

questions.question: Where should I watch for Pilgrammed updates and drops?
questions.answer: Watch the Pilgrammed Discord, the Phexonia Studios Roblox group, the gameplay trailer channel, and the active community spaces whenever a new Wind-era patch is approaching.

cta.title: Ready to Explore Pilgrammed?
cta.description: Join the Pilgrammed Discord community, follow new discoveries, and jump straight into the Roblox adventure.

guide.description: Core Pilgrammed guides for first-hour routing, upgrade order, fishing, trading limits, quests, and long-term progression.

guide.whatIsDescription: Pilgrammed guide articles focus on the systems players touch most often: early routing, quest flow, inventory habits, fishing, progression pacing, and the movement upgrades that unlock safer travel.

whySteps.step1Description: Use beginner, inventory, and loadout guides to avoid wasted detours and build a stable first route.
whySteps.step2Description: Follow quest-order, max-level, and upgrade coverage to move through Pilgrammed with fewer dead ends.
whySteps.step3Description: Keep fishing, parrying, trading, and guild info in one place when you need practical answers fast.

build.title: Pilgrammed Builds
build.description: Compare Pilgrammed stats, classes, techniques, elements, reforges, and role-based builds for mage, tank, healer, and hybrid setups.
build.metaDescription: Plan Pilgrammed builds with stat priorities, class ideas, techniques, reforge choices, and role-focused setups for every stage.
build.whatIsDescription: Pilgrammed build coverage groups together the stat systems and gear choices that define a playstyle, from raw attribute spreads to class identity, elemental synergies, and reforge direction.

whySteps.step1Description: Compare agility, dexterity, intellect, and other core attributes before you lock points into a weak spread.
whySteps.step2Description: Use class, healer, mage, tank, and technique guides to pair your armor and weapons with a real combat role.
whySteps.step3Description: Layer reforges, elements, and hybrid scaling so your Pilgrammed build keeps up in harder boss routes.

weapons.title: Pilgrammed Weapons
weapons.description: Track Pilgrammed weapon rankings, early-game upgrades, signature drops, and best-in-slot picks across melee, ranged, and magic.
weapons.metaTitle: Pilgrammed Weapons - Tier List, Best Picks & Early Gear
weapons.metaDescription: Browse Pilgrammed weapons, from early-game upgrades to tier-list favorites, bows, daggers, guns, and standout endgame gear.
weapons.whatIsDescription: The Pilgrammed weapons hub collects both broad ranking articles and single-weapon breakdowns, making it easier to compare move sets, progression value, and role fit before you invest resources.

whySteps.step1Description: See which early and mid-game weapons are worth chasing instead of overcommitting to starter gear.
whySteps.step2Description: Use tier lists and best-weapon guides to weigh damage, range, safety, and technique value side by side.
whySteps.step3Description: Jump into focused pages for bows, daggers, guns, and named drops when you need details on one weapon family.

bosses.title: Pilgrammed Bosses
bosses.description: Follow Pilgrammed boss guides for arena routes, key attacks, cheese options, useful drops, and progression checkpoints.
bosses.metaDescription: Browse Pilgrammed boss guides covering locations, attacks, farming value, drops, and the safest ways to win major fights.
bosses.whatIsDescription: Pilgrammed boss pages are encounter files built around the questions players care about most: where the arena is, what the boss actually does, what it drops, and whether the fight is worth farming now.

whySteps.step1Description: Get to bosses faster with clear location context, route notes, and nearby checkpoints before the fight starts.
whySteps.step2Description: Review the attacks and punish windows that matter instead of learning every mechanic through repeated deaths.
whySteps.step3Description: Use drop and progression notes to decide which Pilgrammed bosses deserve your time at each stage.

items.title: Pilgrammed Items
items.description: Review Pilgrammed armor, accessories, gems, shields, potions, and utility gear that support real combat and farming decisions.
items.metaTitle: Pilgrammed Items - Armor, Gems & Utility Gear
items.metaDescription: Explore Pilgrammed items including armor, accessories, gems, shields, potions, and special drops that shape builds and routes.
items.whatIsDescription: The Pilgrammed items section is where equipment and utility coverage meets. It brings together armor, accessories, consumables, upgrade materials, and special drops that support both combat builds and world traversal.

whySteps.step1Description: Compare armor, shields, and accessories before you spend time farming a piece that does not fit your build.
whySteps.step2Description: Keep gems, lanterns, potions, and niche tools organized so you know what each item actually solves.
whySteps.step3Description: Use focused item pages for rings, fragments, and rare materials when a single pickup matters to your next upgrade.

locations.title: Pilgrammed Locations
locations.description: Navigate Pilgrammed locations with region overviews, waypoint routes, landmark notes, and travel shortcuts across the world map.
locations.metaTitle: Pilgrammed Locations - Map Routes, Landmarks & Waypoints
locations.metaDescription: Find Pilgrammed locations, from major regions and hidden paths to waypoint routes, landmarks, and farming shortcuts.
locations.whatIsDescription: Pilgrammed location guides map the spaces between fights. They cover regions, sub-areas, waypoints, landmarks, and the traversal decisions that make farming loops and boss runs less confusing.

whySteps.step1Description: Use landmark-based directions to reach areas like the Volcano, Catacombs, and Kings Den without trial-and-error wandering.
whySteps.step2Description: Connect waypoints, checkpoints, and farming spots into cleaner movement paths across the Pilgrammed map.
whySteps.step3Description: Read area notes before you enter a zone so you know what enemies, rewards, and progression hooks are waiting there.

codes.title: Pilgrammed Codes
codes.description: Check the Pilgrammed codes status, redemption availability, and the official channels most likely to publish the first real public drop.
codes.metaDescription: Track Pilgrammed codes status, redemption availability, and the official watchlist for the first public code release.
codes.whatIsDescription: The Pilgrammed codes page is a status hub for a category that players search for constantly even though the game has not settled into a normal public redeem cycle. It tracks whether codes exist, whether a redeem flow is live, and where official drops would surface first.

whySteps.step1Description: See whether Pilgrammed currently has working public codes or an active redemption flow before wasting time on fake lists.
whySteps.step2Description: Keep the Roblox group, Discord, and update windows in view so you know where the first real code is likely to appear.
whySteps.step3Description: Use one clean Pilgrammed watchlist instead of bouncing between outdated redeem pages and copycat code claims.
